hooks.prompt:
Before stopping, verify these phase-gate-sequence checks. Answer YES only if ALL pass, otherwise list what is failing:
1. Are there no uncommitted changes? (run: git status --porcelain)
2. Does today's scratchpad have a '## Pre-Compact Checkpoint' entry since the last ## Phase header?
3. Have all changed scripts passed ruff check? (uv run ruff check scripts/ tests/ --quiet)
4. If any docs/research/*.md was changed: does it have Status: Final and is every ## Recommendations item tracked as a #NNN issue or marked deferred?
If stop_hook_active is set, skip this check and proceed immediately.

Write a '## Pre-Compact Checkpoint' section to the active scratchpad (at .tmp/<branch>/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md) capturing: (1) active phase name and status, (2) all deliverables committed so far with commit SHAs, (3) the single next concrete step, (4) any open blockers. Keep it under 200 words.

Context was just compacted. Before continuing: (1) read the active scratchpad from disk: cat .tmp/<branch-slug>/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).md, (2) read the active workplan from docs/plans/ if one exists, (3) run git log --oneline -5 and git status to confirm committed vs in-flight state. Only then write '## Post-Compact Reorientation' and continue.

Session is ending. Complete these session close steps:
1. Write '## Session Summary' to the active scratchpad — include: date range, branch, PR if open, all commits (SHA + one-line desc), issues closed, lessons learned, recommended next session action.
2. Post a progress comment (gh issue comment <N> --body-file /tmp/comment_N.md) on every GitHub issue actively worked this session.
3. Confirm all commits are pushed: git status && git log --oneline -5.
4. Report: 'Session close complete — summary written, N comments posted, branch status: [clean/uncommitted changes]'.
